Class FlightBoard

java.lang.Object
Model.Board.FlightBoard
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Cloneable
public class FlightBoard extends Object implements Serializable, Cloneable
FlightBoard: it holds all the good stuff you need during the building and the flying phase.

This class would really benefit from getting split into LearnerFlightBoard and LevelTwoFlightBoard. Why? In the learner flight, specifically in the building phase, timer doesn't exist (see page 4 of galaxy-trucker-rules-it.pdf), the hiddenCardDeck is technically not formed yet (see page 9) and there is no such thing as a peekableCardDeck. Regardless of the phase, the number of cells differs, as does the layout, and it would overall be a good idea to abstract this better.

Also would benefit if split into FlightBoard and BuildingFlightBoard (or BuildingBoard). Why? timer, hiddenCardDeck and peekableCardDecks do not exist while flying (but this is a less pressing issue than the one stated earlier).

See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • FlightBoard

      public FlightBoard()

    • FlightBoard

      public FlightBoard(CardDeck hiddenCards) throws AssertionError
      FlightBoard constructor of learner level. Sets the number of cells to 18. Sets the hidden card deck to the passed value. Sets the player positions map to an empty HashMap. Sets every other member to null.
      Parameters:
      hiddenCards - the deck of cards to be used later in the flight phase
      Throws:
      AssertionError - if something is wrong with passed deck: the number of cards is not 8, the level of the cards is not LEARNER...
      See Also:
      • CardDeck
      • AssertionError

    • FlightBoard

      public FlightBoard(CardDeck hiddenCardDeck, List<CardDeck> peekableCardDecks)
      FlightBoard constructor of level 2. Sets the number of cells to 24. Sets the timer to a new Timer. Sets the peekable card decks to the passed value. Sets the hidden card deck to the passed value. Sets the player positions map to an empty HashMap. Sets every other member to null.
      Parameters:
      hiddenCardDeck - the deck of cards to be used later in the flight phase
      peekableCardDecks - the list of peekable card decks
      Throws:
      AssertionError - if something is wrong with passed decks: peekableCardDecks size is not 3, peekableCardDecks elements size is not 4...

    • FlightBoard

      public FlightBoard(List<Player> players, CardDeck deck, MatchLevel level)

    • FlightBoard

      private FlightBoard(FlightBoard old)

  • Method Details

    • main

      public static void main(String[] args)
      FlightBoard default constructor stub.

      Now always throws because: How can I know if it's supposed to be a learner or a level 2 flightboard? How can I make such assumption? Should I make such an assumption?

      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - always

    • setUpcomingCardDeck

      public boolean setUpcomingCardDeck()
      Sets the upcoming card deck by combining all peekable card decks and the hidden card deck, shuffling them, and clearing the peekable card decks and the hidden card deck.
      Returns:
      true if the upcoming card deck was successfully set, false if the timer is not in the last phase with no time left

    • getPosition

      public int getPosition(Player player) throws NullPointerException
      Returns the position of the specified player, or null if no position was found for such player.
      Parameters:
      player - the player whose associated position is to be returned
      Returns:
      the position to which the specified player is mapped, or null if this map contains no position for such player
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the specified key is null

    • getTotalDistance

      public int getTotalDistance(Player player) throws NullPointerException
      Throws:
      NullPointerException

      public void updatePosition(Player player, int position)

      public void setStartingPositions(Player player, int position) throws InvalidMethodParameters
      Sets the starting positions of the players.
      Parameters:
      player -
      position -
      Throws:
      InvalidMethodParameters

    • deltaFlightDays

      public void deltaFlightDays(Player player, int deltaDays) throws InvalidMethodParameters
      Moves the player by the specified number of days. If the number of days is negative, the player moves backwards. If the number of days is positive, the player moves forwards.
      Parameters:
      player - the player to move
      deltaDays - the number of days to move
      Throws:
      InvalidMethodParameters - if the player is not found in the flight board
